seoTitle: Infineon Technical Support | Selection Guides, Application Notes, FAQ | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Technical support resources for Infineon products: selection guides, application notes, troubleshooting guides, product reviews, and FAQ. Expert FAE support available.

faqs.question: How can I verify that BeiLuo Electronics is an authorized Infineon distributor?
faqs.answer: Verify our authorization through multiple channels: Infineon Official Website - Check Infineon's distributor locator on www.infineon.com where BeiLuo Electronics is listed as a core distributor; Authorization Certificate - We can provide current Infineon distributor authorization certificate upon request; Direct Confirmation - Contact Infineon directly to verify our distributor status using our company information; Documentation - All Infineon products from BeiLuo include original manufacturer certificates of conformance and traceability documentation. As a core distributor, we have direct supply agreements with Infineon and access to their full product portfolio, technical resources, and factory support. Our 20+ year partnership ensures reliable supply and competitive pricing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team to request authorization documentation or place your order with confidence in our genuine Infineon products.

faqs.question: What is the minimum order quantity (MOQ) for Infineon products?
faqs.answer: Infineon product MOQ policies: Standard Products - Many popular IGBT modules and MOSFETs have no MOQ; single piece orders accepted for evaluation and small production; Volume Pricing - Price breaks typically at 100, 500, 1000, and 5000+ pieces; higher volumes receive preferential pricing; Custom Products - Specialized configurations or high-voltage modules may have MOQ of 50-100 pieces due to manufacturing batch sizes; Samples - Free samples available for qualified projects and volume production plans; contact FAE for sample requests; Bonded Inventory - For high-volume customers, we offer bonded inventory programs with no MOQ for scheduled releases. Stock items like FF300R12ME4, BSC028N04LS, and TC399XX can be ordered in any quantity. Contact sales for specific MOQ and pricing for your target products.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request a quote for your required quantities - we accommodate orders from single pieces to high-volume production.

faqs.question: How do I request a quote for Infineon products?
faqs.answer: Request a quote through multiple convenient channels: Online Form - Submit RFQ through our website contact form with part numbers and quantities; Email - Send your BOM to hk@elec-distributor.com with project details and target delivery; Phone - Call +86 15013702378 for immediate assistance with urgent requirements; WhatsApp/WeChat - Message us for quick quotes on standard products. Information Needed - Part numbers, quantities, required delivery date, target price (if known), end application, and annual forecast for volume pricing. Response Time - Standard products: 1-2 business days; special configurations: 3-5 business days. Volume Pricing - We offer competitive tiered pricing and can match or beat competitor pricing for qualified opportunities. Technical Support - Our FAE team can review your BOM for optimization opportunities and alternative recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Submit your RFQ now through any channel above - our team is ready to provide competitive pricing and delivery schedules.

faqs.question: What are the typical lead times for Infineon products?
faqs.answer: Infineon product lead times vary by product type: In-Stock Items - Popular products (FF300R12ME4, BSC028N04LS, XMC4700) available for same-day or next-day shipping from our warehouse; Standard Lead Time - 4-8 weeks for most IGBT modules and MOSFETs from Infineon factory; Long Lead Time - Specialized high-voltage modules (>1700V), automotive-grade MCUs, and custom configurations: 12-16 weeks; Expedited Delivery - Air freight options available for urgent requirements at additional cost; Bonded Inventory - For high-volume customers, we maintain dedicated stock with immediate availability. Current market conditions may affect lead times. Contact us for real-time availability and lead time confirmation for your specific requirements. We recommend forecasting and placing orders in advance for critical production schedules.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact sales for current lead times and stock availability, or inquire about our bonded inventory program for guaranteed supply.

faqs.question: What payment terms does BeiLuo Electronics offer?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo Electronics offers flexible payment terms: Standard Terms - T/T (Wire Transfer) in advance for first-time customers; established customers may qualify for Net 30 terms after credit approval; Payment Methods - Bank wire transfer (T/T), Letter of Credit (L/C) for large orders, and secure online payment options; Currency - USD, EUR, CNY (RMB) accepted; Credit Terms - Net 30-60 available for qualified customers with credit application and references; Volume Customers - Special payment terms and pricing for high-volume, long-term partnerships; Deposit - Large or custom orders may require 30-50% deposit with balance before shipment. Proforma Invoice - Provided upon request for payment planning and approval processes. All prices are FCA Hong Kong unless otherwise specified. Contact our sales team to discuss payment terms that work for your business.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team to discuss payment terms and establish credit terms for your organization.

faqs.question: How does BeiLuo handle shipping and logistics?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive shipping services: Shipping Methods - DHL, FedEx, UPS for express delivery (2-5 days worldwide); air freight for larger shipments; sea freight for cost-sensitive bulk orders; Packaging - ESD-safe packaging for all semiconductor products; moisture-sensitive devices packed with desiccant and humidity indicators; hazardous materials (batteries) shipped per IATA regulations; Shipping Origins - Hong Kong (primary), Shenzhen, Shanghai depending on product availability and customer location; Customs - We handle export documentation; customers responsible for import duties and taxes in destination country; Insurance - Full-value shipping insurance included; Tracking - Detailed tracking information provided for all shipments; Lead Time - Same-day dispatch for in-stock items with express shipping; scheduled shipments for production orders. Special Requirements - Temperature-controlled shipping available for sensitive components; contact us for special handling requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Specify your preferred shipping method when placing your order, or let us recommend the best option for your delivery requirements.

faqs.question: What is BeiLuo's return and warranty policy for Infineon products?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo Electronics return and warranty policies: Warranty Period - 12 months from date of shipment for standard products; 24-36 months for certain automotive-grade products per manufacturer warranty; Defective Products - Report defects within 30 days of discovery; RMA required for returns; products tested and replaced if manufacturing defect confirmed; Return Conditions - Products must be unused, in original packaging, and returned within 90 days of shipment for standard returns; custom or programmed products non-returnable; Non-Defective Returns - Subject to 15-25% restocking fee depending on product condition and packaging; Process - Contact support to obtain RMA number before returning; include detailed failure description for defective claims; Shipping - Customer pays return shipping; BeiLuo pays replacement shipping for confirmed defects; Limitations - Warranty covers manufacturing defects only; damage from misuse, overvoltage, improper handling, or unauthorized modification not covered.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our support team immediately if you encounter any product issues - we're committed to resolving warranty claims quickly.

faqs.question: How can I identify counterfeit Infineon products?
faqs.answer: Identify counterfeit Infineon products with these checks: Packaging - Authentic products use Infineon-branded anti-static bags with proper logos and part number labels; counterfeits often have poor print quality or generic packaging; Markings - Genuine Infineon products have clear, consistent laser markings; counterfeits may have uneven, misspelled, or smudged markings; Physical Quality - Authentic products have uniform molding, consistent color, and precise dimensions; counterfeits often show poor manufacturing quality; Documentation - Genuine products include manufacturer CoC and traceability documentation; Price - Prices significantly below market rate are a red flag; Source - Only purchase from authorized distributors like BeiLuo Electronics. If you suspect counterfeit products, contact us immediately with photos, batch numbers, and supplier information. We can verify authenticity through Infineon's traceability systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Purchase only from BeiLuo Electronics to ensure 100% genuine Infineon products with full documentation and warranty.

faqs.question: What technical support does BeiLuo provide for Infineon products?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive technical support: FAE Support - Dedicated Field Application Engineers with deep Infineon product expertise; Design Assistance - Schematic review, PCB layout guidance, thermal analysis, and component selection; Application Support - Troubleshooting, failure analysis, and optimization recommendations; Reference Designs - Access to Infineon reference designs and evaluation kits; Documentation - Datasheets, application notes, simulation models, and software libraries; Training - Product training and application workshops available; Response Time - Technical inquiries typically answered within 24-48 hours; On-Site Support - Available for major customers and complex projects. Support Channels - Email: hk@elec-distributor.com, Phone: +86 15013702378, WhatsApp/WeChat available. Our FAE team has extensive experience with motor drives, power supplies, renewable energy, and automotive applications using Infineon components.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical support team for design assistance, troubleshooting, or application guidance for your Infineon-based designs.

faqs.question: Can BeiLuo provide samples for prototyping?
faqs.answer: Yes, BeiLuo Electronics provides samples for qualified projects: Sample Policy - Free samples available for genuine evaluation and production planning; Sample Quantities - Typically 1-5 pieces per part number depending on product value and availability; Qualification - Project information required: end application, estimated annual usage, target production timeline; Lead Time - In-stock samples ship within 1-2 business days; factory samples may take 2-4 weeks; Evaluation Kits - Complete reference design kits available for motor drives, power supplies, and automotive applications; Cost - Standard samples are free of charge; customer pays shipping; special evaluation kits may have nominal cost; Restrictions - Limited to qualified business customers; not available for personal projects or resale. To request samples, contact our FAE team with your project details and required part numbers. We prioritize requests with clear production intent and volume potential.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your project details to request samples for evaluation and prototyping.

faqs.question: How do I get technical documentation for Infineon products?
faqs.answer: Access Infineon technical documentation through multiple channels: BeiLuo Website - Datasheets, application notes, and reference designs available on product pages; Infineon Website - Complete documentation library at www.infineon.com including datasheets, app notes, simulation models, and software; Direct Request - Contact our support team for specific documentation not readily available; Reference Designs - Complete design packages including schematics, PCB layouts, and BOMs available for qualified projects; Simulation Models - SPICE models, PLECS models, and thermal simulation files available for power devices; Software - Driver libraries, example code, and configuration tools for MCUs. All documentation is provided free of charge. For proprietary or restricted documents (detailed reliability reports, etc.), NDA may be required. Our FAE team can help locate specific documents and provide application-specific guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse product pages for datasheets, or contact our support team for specific technical documentation requests.

faqs.question: Does BeiLuo offer volume pricing and long-term supply agreements?
faqs.answer: Yes, BeiLuo Electronics offers comprehensive volume programs: Volume Pricing - Tiered pricing structure: 100+, 500+, 1000+, 5000+, 10000+ pieces with significant discounts at higher volumes; Price Matching - We match or beat competitor pricing for qualified opportunities; Long-Term Agreements (LTA) - Fixed pricing and guaranteed supply for 12-24 month periods; Bonded Inventory - Dedicated stock maintained for your forecasted requirements with scheduled releases; Forecasting - Share your production forecast for allocation priority and better pricing; Consignment - Inventory held at your location or nearby hub, billed upon consumption; Blanket Orders - Place large orders with scheduled deliveries to optimize logistics and pricing. Benefits - Price stability, supply security, priority allocation during shortages, dedicated FAE support. Contact our sales team to discuss your volume requirements and negotiate a customized supply agreement tailored to your business needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team to discuss volume pricing, long-term agreements, and customized supply programs for your production needs.

articles.title: How to Select the Right Infineon MCU for Your Project: A Comprehensive Guide

author.name: John Chen
author.title: Senior FAE - MCU & Automotive
author.bio: John Chen has over 10 years of experience in semiconductor distribution, specializing in automotive and industrial microcontrollers. He holds a Master's degree in Electrical Engineering from Tsinghua University and has supported hundreds of design-in projects.

articles.summary: A comprehensive guide to selecting the optimal Infineon microcontroller based on your application requirements, performance needs, safety certifications, and budget constraints. This guide covers AURIX, XMC, and PSoC families.
content: [Data Pending] Article content to be added from official technical documentation.

downloads.title: AURIX vs XMC vs PSoC Comparison

articles.seoTitle: How to Select Infineon MCU | AURIX vs XMC vs PSoC | Complete Guide | BeiLuo
articles.seoDescription: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right Infineon MCU: AURIX for automotive, XMC for industrial, PSoC for IoT. Performance, memory, peripherals, safety considerations. Expert FAE support. Contact: +86 15013702378

faeInsights.insight: In my decade of supporting microcontroller designs, I've observed that the most successful projects are those that carefully match the MCU capabilities to the application requirements from the start. For automotive applications, the AURIX family's hardware security module and lockstep cores provide essential safety features that are difficult to implement in software alone. For industrial applications, the XMC family's rich analog peripherals and motor control capabilities can significantly reduce system complexity and cost. One common mistake I see is selecting an MCU based solely on processing power, while overlooking the importance of peripheral integration and development ecosystem. A well-chosen MCU with integrated peripherals can eliminate external components and reduce PCB complexity.
faeInsights.logic: MCU selection framework: 1) Define application requirements including safety certification needs (ASIL-D for automotive, SIL for industrial); 2) Determine processing requirements based on control loop frequency and algorithm complexity; 3) Evaluate peripheral needs (ADC channels, PWM outputs, communication interfaces); 4) Consider memory requirements for program code and data logging; 5) Assess development ecosystem and software support; 6) Evaluate long-term availability and roadmap; 7) Consider cost targets and volume projections.
keyTakeaways: Match MCU capabilities to application requirements from project start
Consider safety certification requirements early in selection process
Peripheral integration can significantly reduce system complexity
Development ecosystem quality impacts time-to-market
Long-term availability is critical for industrial applications
commonPitfalls: Selecting MCU based only on clock speed without considering peripheral needs
Underestimating memory requirements for future feature expansion
Overlooking safety certification requirements until late in design
bestPractices: Use vendor-provided software libraries and example code
Implement comprehensive testing including boundary conditions
Design for worst-case timing scenarios
Plan for firmware updates and bootloader implementation
Document hardware-software interface thoroughly
troubleshootingTips: Use debugger to verify peripheral configuration registers
Check clock tree configuration when experiencing timing issues
Verify power supply sequencing for reliable startup
faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience, this How to Select the Right Infineon MCU for Your Project: A Comprehensive Guide article addresses common challenges engineers face in project development.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

customerCases.problem: The customer was developing a BMS for a new electric vehicle platform and needed an MCU that could handle complex battery monitoring algorithms while meeting ASIL-D functional safety requirements. Their initial MCU choice lacked hardware safety features, requiring extensive software-based safety mechanisms that consumed significant processing resources.
customerCases.diagnosis: After reviewing the system architecture, I identified that the AURIX TC3xx family's integrated safety features would significantly simplify the design while improving reliability. The lockstep cores and hardware security module were exactly what was needed for this safety-critical application.
customerCases.solution: We recommended the AURIX TC399XX with its six TriCore processors and dedicated safety cores. The integrated HSM provided secure key storage for cryptographic functions, while the lockstep cores enabled ASIL-D compliance with minimal software overhead. We also provided reference code for BMS applications.
customerCases.results: The customer successfully achieved ASIL-D certification on their first submission, saving an estimated 6 months in development time. The hardware-based safety features reduced software complexity by 40%, and the system achieved excellent battery monitoring accuracy of ±5mV per cell.

articles.title: IGBT Selection Guide for Power Applications: Voltage, Current, and Thermal Considerations

author.name: Michael Wang
author.title: Power Electronics FAE
author.bio: Michael Wang has 8+ years of experience in power electronics design and semiconductor distribution. He specializes in IGBT and MOSFET applications for motor drives, renewable energy, and industrial power conversion.

articles.summary: Learn how to select the optimal IGBT module based on voltage rating, current capability, switching frequency, thermal requirements, and package type. This guide covers practical selection methodology with real-world examples.
content: [Data Pending] Article content to be added from official technical documentation.
[Data Pending] Technical details to be verified with manufacturer.
[Data Pending] Application guidelines to be confirmed by FAE team.
articles.seoTitle: IGBT Selection Guide | Voltage, Current, Thermal | Infineon | BeiLuo
articles.seoDescription: Complete IGBT selection guide: voltage rating, current capability, switching frequency, thermal design, package selection. Practical examples for motor drives, inverters. Expert FAE support. Contact: +86 15013702378

faeInsights.insight: After 15 years of supporting power electronics designs, I've learned that IGBT selection is as much about understanding the application environment as it is about comparing datasheet parameters. The most reliable designs are those that account for real-world conditions: voltage transients from long cables, thermal cycling from intermittent operation, and protection against fault conditions. I always recommend derating devices by at least 30% for current and 20% for voltage to ensure long-term reliability. The gate drive design is equally critical - a well-designed gate drive can make an average IGBT perform excellently, while a poor gate drive can cause the best IGBT to fail prematurely.
faeInsights.logic: IGBT selection and design process: 1) Calculate maximum DC bus voltage and add 50% safety margin for voltage rating; 2) Determine RMS and peak current requirements including overload conditions; 3) Calculate total power losses (conduction + switching) at operating conditions; 4) Design thermal management system based on calculated losses; 5) Select gate driver with appropriate drive capability and protection features; 6) Design gate drive circuit with proper resistor values and layout; 7) Implement comprehensive protection (overcurrent, short-circuit, overtemperature); 8) Validate design through testing including fault conditions.
keyTakeaways: Always include significant voltage and current margins (30-50%)
Gate drive design is as important as IGBT selection
Thermal design must consider worst-case operating conditions
Protection features are essential for reliable operation
Switching frequency impacts both losses and EMI
commonPitfalls: Insufficient gate drive current leading to slow switching and high losses
Inadequate thermal design causing overheating under load
Poor layout creating excessive stray inductance and voltage overshoot
bestPractices: Use recommended gate drivers with integrated protection
Implement soft-start to limit inrush current
Include snubber circuits for voltage spike suppression
Design for easy testing and maintenance access
Use quality thermal interface material and proper mounting torque
troubleshootingTips: Measure gate waveform to verify proper drive signal
Check for voltage overshoot during switching with oscilloscope
Monitor case temperature under full load conditions
faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience, this IGBT Selection Guide for Power Applications: Voltage, Current, and Thermal Considerations article addresses common challenges engineers face in project development.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

customerCases.problem: The customer was experiencing IGBT failures in their 75kW motor drives after only 6-12 months of field operation. The failures appeared random and were causing significant warranty costs and customer dissatisfaction. Initial analysis suggested thermal issues, but the root cause was unclear.
customerCases.diagnosis: I visited the customer's facility and conducted detailed thermal measurements and switching waveform analysis. The investigation revealed that voltage overshoot from long motor cables (over 50m) was exceeding the IGBT's voltage rating during switching, causing cumulative damage. Additionally, the thermal interface material was degrading over thermal cycles.
customerCases.solution: We recommended several design improvements: upgrading to FF300R12ME4 IGBT modules with higher voltage margin, implementing RC snubber circuits to suppress voltage overshoot, improving the thermal interface with phase-change material, and adding the 1ED020I12-F2 gate driver with desaturation protection for fault detection.
customerCases.results: After implementing the recommended changes, the customer achieved zero IGBT failures over 3 years of operation. The improved design also increased efficiency by 2%, reducing energy costs for end users. The customer has since applied these design practices across their entire product line.

articles.seoDescription: Comprehensive IGBT failure analysis: overcurrent, overvoltage, thermal overstress, gate oxide failure. Root causes, prevention strategies, case studies. Expert FAE support. Contact: +86 15013702378

faeInsights.insight: After 15 years of supporting power electronics designs, I've learned that IGBT selection is as much about understanding the application environment as it is about comparing datasheet parameters. The most reliable designs are those that account for real-world conditions: voltage transients from long cables, thermal cycling from intermittent operation, and protection against fault conditions. I always recommend derating devices by at least 30% for current and 20% for voltage to ensure long-term reliability. The gate drive design is equally critical - a well-designed gate drive can make an average IGBT perform excellently, while a poor gate drive can cause the best IGBT to fail prematurely.
faeInsights.logic: IGBT selection and design process: 1) Calculate maximum DC bus voltage and add 50% safety margin for voltage rating; 2) Determine RMS and peak current requirements including overload conditions; 3) Calculate total power losses (conduction + switching) at operating conditions; 4) Design thermal management system based on calculated losses; 5) Select gate driver with appropriate drive capability and protection features; 6) Design gate drive circuit with proper resistor values and layout; 7) Implement comprehensive protection (overcurrent, short-circuit, overtemperature); 8) Validate design through testing including fault conditions.
keyTakeaways: Always include significant voltage and current margins (30-50%)
Gate drive design is as important as IGBT selection
Thermal design must consider worst-case operating conditions
Protection features are essential for reliable operation
Switching frequency impacts both losses and EMI
commonPitfalls: Insufficient gate drive current leading to slow switching and high losses
Inadequate thermal design causing overheating under load
Poor layout creating excessive stray inductance and voltage overshoot
bestPractices: Use recommended gate drivers with integrated protection
Implement soft-start to limit inrush current
Include snubber circuits for voltage spike suppression
Design for easy testing and maintenance access
Use quality thermal interface material and proper mounting torque
troubleshootingTips: Measure gate waveform to verify proper drive signal
Check for voltage overshoot during switching with oscilloscope
Monitor case temperature under full load conditions
faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience, this Common IGBT Failure Modes and Prevention Strategies: A Comprehensive Analysis article addresses common challenges engineers face in project development.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

customerCases.problem: The customer was experiencing IGBT failures in their 75kW motor drives after only 6-12 months of field operation. The failures appeared random and were causing significant warranty costs and customer dissatisfaction. Initial analysis suggested thermal issues, but the root cause was unclear.
customerCases.diagnosis: I visited the customer's facility and conducted detailed thermal measurements and switching waveform analysis. The investigation revealed that voltage overshoot from long motor cables (over 50m) was exceeding the IGBT's voltage rating during switching, causing cumulative damage. Additionally, the thermal interface material was degrading over thermal cycles.
customerCases.solution: We recommended several design improvements: upgrading to FF300R12ME4 IGBT modules with higher voltage margin, implementing RC snubber circuits to suppress voltage overshoot, improving the thermal interface with phase-change material, and adding the 1ED020I12-F2 gate driver with desaturation protection for fault detection.
customerCases.results: After implementing the recommended changes, the customer achieved zero IGBT failures over 3 years of operation. The improved design also increased efficiency by 2%, reducing energy costs for end users. The customer has since applied these design practices across their entire product line.

faq.question: What is the difference between AURIX and XMC microcontrollers?
faq.answer: AURIX is designed for automotive safety-critical applications with ASIL-D compliance, TriCore architecture, and up to 6 cores. XMC targets industrial applications with ARM Cortex-M4F, extensive analog peripherals, and IEC 61508 SIL2 certification. Choose AURIX for automotive, XMC for industrial motor control and power conversion.

faq.question: How do I select the right IGBT voltage rating?
faq.answer: Select IGBT voltage rating with at least 1.5-2× margin over maximum expected voltage. For 400VAC input (540VDC), use 1200V IGBT. For 230VAC input (325VDC), 650V IGBT is sufficient. Always consider voltage spikes from switching transients.

faq.question: What gate drive voltage should I use for Infineon IGBTs?
faq.answer: Recommended gate drive voltage is +15V for turn-on and -5V for turn-off. The negative turn-off voltage prevents parasitic turn-on from Miller effect. For low-power applications, 0V turn-off is acceptable but not recommended for bridge configurations.

faq.question: Can I parallel IGBT modules for higher current?
faq.answer: Yes, IGBT modules can be paralleled. Key considerations: matched Vce(sat) characteristics, symmetrical layout for equal current sharing, individual gate resistors for each IGBT, and adequate thermal coupling. Infineon IGBTs have positive temperature coefficient for natural current sharing.

faq.question: What is the advantage of SiC MOSFETs over IGBTs?
faq.answer: SiC MOSFETs offer 4× lower switching losses, zero reverse recovery charge, higher operating temperature (175°C vs 150°C), and higher switching frequency capability. Ideal for solar inverters, EV chargers, and high-frequency power supplies. IGBTs remain cost-effective for lower frequency applications.

faq.question: How do I protect IGBTs from short-circuit conditions?
faq.answer: Use desaturation detection to detect overcurrent within 3-10μs. Implement soft shutdown to limit voltage spikes during fault turn-off. Add fast-acting fuses for catastrophic faults. Ensure proper dead time in bridge configurations to prevent shoot-through.

faq.question: What is the typical lifetime of Infineon IGBT modules?
faq.answer: Infineon IGBT modules are designed for 100,000+ hours of operation (over 10 years) when operated within specifications. Key factors affecting lifetime: junction temperature (keep Tj <125°C), thermal cycling (minimize ΔT), and proper mounting/cooling.

glossary.definition: Automotive Safety Integrity Level - ISO 26262 safety classification (ASIL-A to ASIL-D)
Desaturation detection - IGBT overcurrent protection method
Field-Oriented Control - Advanced motor control algorithm
Maximum Power Point Tracking - Solar inverter optimization algorithm
Reverse recovery charge - Charge stored in diode during conduction
Collector-emitter saturation voltage - IGBT conduction voltage drop
Under-Voltage Lockout - Protection against insufficient gate drive voltage
